title: Fakin It

jokes.text: Well, they've got one guy who won't be talking. That is, unless there's a hand inside of him.
jokes.setup: Michael talks about someone who won't be talking at the trial
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's referring to Gob's puppet Franklin, not a person
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection with tight structure - sets up dramatic tension then undercuts with absurd reality

jokes.text: Oh, please, Michael. Even then, I wouldn't say anything.
jokes.setup: Michael's concern about someone talking with a hand inside them
jokes.punchline: George Sr. misses the puppet reference and takes it literally
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Sr.'s self-absorption and missing obvious context

jokes.text: He is re-dyed, rested and ready to work.
jokes.setup: Gob talking about Franklin being ready
jokes.punchline: Uses 'rested and ready' cliché but with 're-dyed' for a Black puppet
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reveals Gob's casual inappropriateness while maintaining his obliviousness

jokes.text: I think he meant 'get Franklin'.
jokes.setup: Gob says he has to tell Franklin about being called as witness
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry correction about retrieving an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Michael's straight-man role perfectly executed with economical correction

jokes.text: Franklin, guess what? / But I don't know anything. / He certainly hasn't heard anything from me.
jokes.setup: Gob tries to tell Franklin news
jokes.punchline: Immediate denial from the puppet, then Gob agrees the puppet knows nothing
jokes.explanation: Absurd puppet conversation that reveals Gob's ventriloquism confusion and self-incrimination

jokes.text: Your father says he wants me to go all the way to Fallujah. I thought he meant the sex act that's so popular with your generation.
jokes.setup: Lucille mentions going to Fallujah
jokes.punchline: She thought it was a sexual position
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - combines her sheltered worldview, generational disconnect, and casual inappropriateness

jokes.text: I know you're awake. Faker!
jokes.setup: Lucille telling drunk secrets to Buster
jokes.punchline: Accuses him of faking sleep while literally talking to someone unconscious
jokes.explanation: Reveals Lucille's paranoia and sets up the episode's faking theme

jokes.text: Whoa. I did not say that. / You did, Gob. Your mouth moved. Your hand didn't.
jokes.setup: Gob denies saying something
jokes.punchline: Michael points out his ventriloquism failure
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Gob's incompetence at his supposed profession

jokes.text: Yet you refer to her as a she. / He, she, what's the difference? / Hear, hear. In the dark it all looks the same.
jokes.setup: Discussion about mysterious N. Bluth
jokes.punchline: Lucille's inappropriately sexual comment about gender differences
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille inappropriate overshare that reveals too much about her marriage

jokes.text: Not another media whore. / Like to see a whore that does that! / Not the carrying the weight part, but, on the other hand, if it's free.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Jan Eagleman from Justice TV
jokes.punchline: Lucille's crude commentary on prostitution economics
jokes.explanation: Lucille's classless commentary feels forced compared to her better moments

jokes.text: You know, Michael, just because you played a role, doesn't make it real. Of course, Buster did grow up to be Captain Hook.
jokes.setup: Lucille dismisses Michael's legal aspirations
jokes.punchline: Accidentally validates her own point with Buster's hook hand
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-defeating argument that works on multiple levels and references show mythology

jokes.text: Not cool, Mom.
jokes.setup: Lucille's Captain Hook reference
jokes.punchline: Buster's mild reaction to traumatic reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster understatement - polite objection to reference to his dismemberment

jokes.text: I'm doing a tech rehearsal for my new show, Judge Reinhold. It's a reality courtroom show. / You know that there's an actor by that name, right?
jokes.setup: Jan explains her show name
jokes.punchline: Michael points out the obvious naming problem
jokes.explanation: Absurd premise well-executed with Michael as perfect straight man

jokes.text: This is what they put me in. Why did I lose all the weight if they're gonna put me in pool cover?
jokes.setup: Jan showing off her judge's robe
jokes.punchline: Compares judicial robes to pool covers
jokes.explanation: Good visual comparison that establishes Jan's vanity and TV background

jokes.text: I read somewhere Judge Judy made $25 million last year. And I'm like, 'Hey, I never even heard of the guy.'
jokes.setup: Jan mentions Judge Judy's success
jokes.punchline: Thinks Judge Judy is a man
jokes.explanation: Establishes Jan's ignorance and opportunism effectively

jokes.text: So, you're doing this because your name is Judge? / Honestly, this is the first time I put those two things together.
jokes.setup: Michael asks about her motivation
jokes.punchline: Jan admits she never connected her name to her show concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: My name is Judge.
jokes.setup: Magazine talking ad activated
jokes.punchline: Repeats Jan's name in robotic voice
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag that will pay off throughout episode

jokes.text: I'll show him. I'll make a fool out of him.
jokes.setup: Michael wants to prove himself as lawyer
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals his true motivation is revenge
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's underlying resentment

jokes.text: I just pray it's one of those things where he's unconscious throughout the entire trial, and when it's over, he wakes up and gets a big toy.
jokes.setup: Lucille expressing concern about Buster's coma
jokes.punchline: Compares serious medical emergency to dental visit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - completely inappropriate comparison that reveals her childlike understanding of consequences

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Are you responsible for this, Mom? Did you put one of your sons into a coma so that he can't testify?
jokes.setup: Buster's convenient coma timing
jokes.punchline: Michael directly accuses his mother of attempted murder
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation that feels earned given the family dynamics

jokes.text: Michael, how dare you? Of course it crossed my mind, but this was all Buster.
jokes.setup: Lucille's indignant response to accusation
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits she considered it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - fake outrage immediately undercut by honest admission

jokes.text: Hope you don't mind me saying, but these things can go on indefinitely. My mother's been in that bed for two years. / How awful. / We don't have a private room.
jokes.setup: Brian shares tragic story about his comatose mother
jokes.punchline: The tragedy is lack of privacy, not the coma itself
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing character's skewed priorities

jokes.text: Looks a little bit more like Red Prawn, Dad.
jokes.setup: George Sr. wearing red costume for Shrimp Fest
jokes.punchline: Visual pun on 'red prawn' vs his intended look
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag but relatively minor

jokes.text: That's the same representation Captain Hook had.
jokes.setup: Learning Michael will be his lawyer
jokes.punchline: References Buster's Peter Pan role and current hook hand
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that works on multiple levels and shows George Sr.'s wit

jokes.text: It's so good to laugh again. / Feels good. / It does. / They say seven minutes heals all wounds.
jokes.setup: Family sharing a laugh together
jokes.punchline: Lucille's malapropism of 'time heals all wounds'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lucille's tendency toward nonsensical wisdom

jokes.text: Wait, not the Judge Reinhold show from all those magazine ads? They're all over the place. Look at this. / My name is Judge.
jokes.setup: George Sr. mentions seeing the ads everywhere
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates by accidentally triggering one
jokes.explanation: Good payoff of running gag with physical comedy element

jokes.text: I could use this in my ventriloquism act. / They have a bar here?
jokes.setup: Gob excited about talking magazine technology
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by mention of hospital bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment showing his short attention span and priorities

jokes.text: Well, Buster's in what we call a light-to-no-coma. In layman's terms, it might be considered a very heavy nap.
jokes.setup: Doctor giving medical update on Buster
jokes.punchline: Minimizes coma to 'heavy nap'
jokes.explanation: Excellent medical euphemism that sounds professional while being absurd

jokes.text: No, that's him.
jokes.setup: Doctor notes lack of brain activity in comatose patient
jokes.punchline: Lucille confirms this is normal for Buster
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character integration - brutal maternal assessment delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I don't care if it takes from now till the end of Shrimp Fest.
jokes.setup: Michael declaring family devotion to staying at hospital
jokes.punchline: Sets extremely short timeframe (festival ends soon)
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's unintentional time limitation

jokes.text: Would I have to touch the old people? / Only their hearts. You could be a groom. / I don't have a girlfriend. / Sister, then. Or a cousin.
jokes.setup: Nurse suggesting George Michael volunteer for fake weddings
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests incestuous options
jokes.explanation: Sets up episode's fake wedding plot with inappropriate suggestion that becomes relevant

jokes.text: Of course, you're gonna have to kiss. / Guess who liked that idea? / He says he doesn't want to do it.
jokes.setup: Narrator saying George Michael liked the kissing idea
jokes.punchline: George Michael immediately says he doesn't want to do it
jokes.explanation: Good narrator misdirection showing George Michael's conflicted feelings

jokes.text: I mean, the girl I would want to give, and she'll want to give, too. And we'll do that together. It's for them, for the sake of... I just... It's a great day... For being sad.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to explain his motivation for fake wedding
jokes.punchline: Completely breaks down in confused stammering
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy showing George Michael's inability to articulate his conflicted feelings

jokes.text: I love soup. If the only thing I could do was lay in bed all day and eat soup, I'd be happy. I wouldn't even have to taste it. I could just take it through a tube. That would actually be better, 'cause I wouldn't even burn my mouth.
jokes.setup: Lindsay defending Buster's supposed desire to stay in coma
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate justification for vegetative state
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lindsay moment - perfectly captures her shallow hedonism and complete lack of empathy

jokes.text: Never let me die.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's elaborate justification for coma life
jokes.punchline: Concludes with passionate plea for life support
jokes.explanation: Perfect button to Lindsay's speech - completely contradicts her earlier point with total conviction

jokes.text: My name is Judge. / Whose name is Judge? / My name is. / Okay. / That's a silly name. / Judge my name. / Yes, I am judging your name. It am silly. / Is. / Please stop. / Now you're correcting my grammar?
jokes.setup: Gob practicing ventriloquism with talking magazine
jokes.punchline: Gets into argument with inanimate advertisement
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of running gag showing Gob's complete confusion about reality

jokes.text: Judge. / He's right, his name is Judge now.
jokes.setup: Magazine continues saying 'Judge'
jokes.punchline: Gob accepts that the ad has renamed itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic - completely accepts absurd premise as reality

jokes.text: Nichael Bluth? Am I N. Bluth? Is he trying to set me up? If I'm N. Bluth, I'm going to prison.
jokes.setup: Michael discovers his misspelled birth certificate
jokes.punchline: Paranoid spiral about father setting him up
jokes.explanation: Good plot advancement with Michael's characteristic paranoia about his father

jokes.text: Nichael was worried.
jokes.setup: Standard narrator transition
jokes.punchline: Uses the misspelled name from birth certificate
jokes.explanation: Clever narrator callback to the misspelling with perfect economy

jokes.text: Well, I guess your dad must've had these from way back when, huh? / My name is... / That seems kind of current.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to explain dad's bodybuilding magazines
jokes.punchline: Talking ad reveals magazines are recent
jokes.explanation: Good use of running gag to reveal character information about Tobias

jokes.text: And so, like many people who no longer felt anything for each other, they decided to get married.
jokes.setup: George Michael and Maeby agreeing to fake wedding
jokes.punchline: Narrator's cynical observation about real marriage
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with perfect narrator timing

jokes.text: Close to Christmas. Was that hard?
jokes.setup: George Sr. notes Michael's birthday date
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Christmas birthday disappointment
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation delivered with characteristic George Sr. insensitivity.

jokes.text: For God's sake, it's a typo, Michael. What kind of father do you think I am? / That is my birthday. / Close to Christmas. Was that hard?
jokes.setup: George Sr. dismissing birth certificate errors
jokes.punchline: Deflects to Christmas birthday complaint
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Sr.'s deflection tactics

jokes.text: Why can't you be like Buster? He put himself in a coma to protect this family, poor kid.
jokes.setup: George Sr. frustrated with Michael's lack of family loyalty
jokes.punchline: Praises Buster's attempted suicide as family devotion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - George Sr.'s twisted view of family loyalty perfectly executed

jokes.text: Shrimp Fest is over, Pop. / Really? Better go to Chili's.
jokes.setup: Michael informing dad the festival ended
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s immediate pivot to chain restaurant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Sr.'s simple pleasures

jokes.text: Show me your sign. / No, the other side. / Tobias, Brian and I believe in the dignity of every human being. Now, why don't we leave the fruits with the vegetables, and go get a coffee?
jokes.setup: Father showing his protest sign
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's inappropriate comment while claiming to value human dignity
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Lindsay's contradictory values

jokes.text: Now, why don't we leave the fruits with the vegetables, and go get a coffee?
jokes.setup: Father Ben suggesting they leave the protest
jokes.punchline: Potentially homophobic comment disguised as innocent phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre that reveals Father Ben's possible homophobia while maintaining plausible deniability.

jokes.text: I can't do this. I can't do this. / But we're not... / What am I doing?
jokes.setup: Fake wedding ceremony proceeding
jokes.punchline: Maeby's panic attack at altar
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the emotional weight of fake ceremony

jokes.text: Now it's a bar mitzvah.
jokes.setup: Maeby runs away from wedding
jokes.punchline: George Michael's calm assessment of situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael deadpan - completely logical response to absurd situation

jokes.text: Now it's a bar mitzvah.
jokes.setup: Maeby runs away leaving George Michael alone
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact description of the ceremony change
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist observation delivered with perfect deadpan timing.

jokes.text: Patient presented with gastric bacterial infection coupled with peptic ulcers. Tetracycline should not be administered due to allergies.
jokes.setup: Gob's new ventriloquism act with medical tape
jokes.punchline: Dry medical diagnosis as puppet dialogue
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept - medical tape as entertainment, very original.

jokes.text: Wow. Sounds like you could use some tricycling.
jokes.setup: Medical recording mentions tetracycline
jokes.punchline: Gob's confused attempt at wordplay
jokes.explanation: Good Gob moment showing his failed attempt at clever wordplay

jokes.text: You were gonna say 'mockery,' weren't you? / I was in trouble, like, three words into that.
jokes.setup: Gob anticipating Michael's criticism about mock trial
jokes.punchline: Michael admits he was heading toward obvious pun
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment about obvious wordplay with honest character beat

jokes.text: Read the Patriot Act. Anything said in a mock trial or daytime courtroom show can be used in any real criminal proceeding or primetime procedural show, subject to the interpretation of the presiding judge or the executive producer.
jokes.setup: Wayne explaining legal ramifications of mock trial
jokes.punchline: Conflates real law with TV production rules
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of both legal system and reality TV with absurd conflation

jokes.text: In our case, Judge Reinhold is both. / Did he get E.P? / Sure did. / Good for him.
jokes.setup: Learning Judge Reinhold is both judge and executive producer
jokes.punchline: Michael's supportive response despite conflict of interest
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's reflexive politeness

jokes.text: I'm trying to get on with my life. / Yeah, so is what remains of Buster.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining about hospital delays
jokes.punchline: Brian's dark reminder about his mother's vegetative state
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works well with characters' self-absorption

jokes.text: Well, just fake it! / Coma.
jokes.setup: Lindsay telling Brian to fake feelings
jokes.punchline: Buster's one-word response breaks his fake coma
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy moment - Buster's subconscious response to Lindsay's advice reveals his deception

jokes.text: Wow, thank you. That's the first time I ever got a standing ovation before the show.
jokes.setup: Judge Reinhold enters to courtroom protocol
jokes.punchline: Misinterprets legal requirement as appreciation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing TV personality confusion about legal protocol

jokes.text: They're not clapping. / They don't in a courtroom. / Please be seated.
jokes.setup: Explaining why there's no applause
jokes.punchline: Reality of courtroom vs TV show expectations
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for Judge Reinhold's TV confusion

jokes.text: Well, I spend so much time making sweet love on my wife that it's hard to hear anything over the clatter of her breasts
jokes.setup: Tobias asked about family discussions
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate and anatomically confused sexual boast
jokes.explanation: Peak Tobias - combines his sexual insecurity, inappropriate oversharing, and medical terminology confusion

jokes.text: And if this were the real trial, I'd cry here. I'm saving my good stuff, too.
jokes.setup: Lucille giving testimony about Buster
jokes.punchline: Admits to calculating emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - complete honesty about her manipulation tactics

jokes.text: My name is Judge. / What kind of name is that, magazine? / I'm just gonna go ahead and jump in right here. I have nothing for him.
jokes.setup: Gob's ventriloquism act with magazine
jokes.punchline: Complete confusion and Michael's immediate surrender
jokes.explanation: Good payoff of running gag with Michael's professional defeat

jokes.text: Well, it looks like we've got a mistrial. But on the plus side, we've also got a hung jury.
jokes.setup: Trial ending in chaos
jokes.punchline: Judge Reinhold makes inappropriate pun about hung jury
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Judge Reinhold's inappropriate TV persona.

jokes.text: But it's just a mock trial. Why didn't you just take it to the grand jury? / Wanted to prove to you that I could do it. / I'm going to a grand jury with that now. / Actually, Michael had no idea that's what he was supposed to do.
jokes.setup: Judge questions Michael's legal strategy
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals Michael's ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Michael's legal confidence with narrator honesty

jokes.text: For, as it turns out, the fake priest was called to a patient's room, and Father Ben filled in, not knowing it was fake. / Congratulations. / And that's how the kids got married.
jokes.setup: Explanation of priest substitution
jokes.punchline: Accidental real marriage from fake ceremony
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - perfect AD twist where fake becomes real with major consequences

jokes.text: For, as it turns out, the fake priest was called to a patient's room, and Father Ben filled in, not knowing it was fake.
jokes.setup: George Michael and Maeby's fake wedding
jokes.punchline: The replacement priest made it accidentally real
jokes.explanation: Brilliant plot twist that creates actual consequences from what should have been harmless.

jokes.text: Hard to sit when you got someone's hand up your ass.
jokes.setup: Bud Cort's court show with puppet
jokes.punchline: Puppet makes inappropriate comment about ventriloquism
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about ventriloquism with sexual innuendo.

jokes.text: Hard to sit when you got someone's hand up your ass. / All right, let's lose the puppet.
jokes.setup: Judge Bud Cort calls for order
jokes.punchline: Franklin's crude but accurate description of ventriloquism
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about the nature of puppetry with immediate consequences

jokes.text: I have an older sister? / So much for not abandoning family.
jokes.setup: Michael discovers Nellie Bluth exists
jokes.punchline: Narrator notes the family abandoning theme
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode conclusion tying together the abandonment theme with plot revelation

tmdb_overview: George, Sr. hires a new attorney who asks the Bluths to participate in a mock trial to help prepare them for the real thing. The attorney gets actor Judge Reinhold to preside over the mock case. Meanwhile, Buster fakes a coma to get out of testifying, and George Michael and Maeby participate in a mock wedding to entertain hospital patients. Back at the office, Michael discovers that he may have a long-lost sister named Nellie Bluth.
insight: Season three peaks at 86.5 with 69 jokes in pure character-comedy mode.
